January 19 | HIGH-CONFIDENCE PICK
NBA (8:00pm ES*)
Pistons -2.5 (-120) = 2.5U

The Collective Analysis
(Combined reasoning from top cappers)

• Defensive Control and Shot Suppression:
  Detroit is coming off a dominant defensive performance against Indiana, holding the Pacers to 35% from the field and 23% from three while forcing 25 turnovers, reinforcing why the Pistons own one of the league’s top-3 scoring defenses. Boston also defended well in its last game, but that came against an Atlanta team that shot just 37% overall. Detroit has now held opponents under 40% shooting in its most recent outing and has driven the Under in 9 straight games, showing a consistent ability to dictate pace and efficiency. That defensive edge supports Detroit controlling this matchup at home and covering a reduced -2.5 number.
• Interior Advantage and Rebounding Edge:
  Detroit’s frontcourt advantage is a recurring theme in this matchup. The Pistons routed Indiana while consistently closing out defensive possessions, and that rebounding strength has been highlighted as a key separator against Boston. The Celtics are coming off a game where they grabbed 55 rebounds, but that was against Atlanta, not a Detroit team built around size and physicality. With Jayson Tatum out and Boston lacking
